calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-10-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for Petitioner.

Mr. Ferdon presented oral argument to support his position with regard to the Court Examiner's request to provide a supplemental declaration. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-27-2009 Continued To Nov-10-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar Jul-15-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Malone for Edmund Carney. Nancy Rasch for James Ward. Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator.

Mr. Malone stated that there were 23 separate heirs with 5 different attorneys and that they had reached a consensus on who the heirs were and the proportionate distributions. In addition, Mr. Malone presented an order for the court to sign which had been reviewed by Ms. Rasch and Mr. Ferdon. The Court granted the petition, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR#7489. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F.Smith.

Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar Jul-08-2009 Continued To Jul-15-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar May-05-2009 Continued To Jul-08-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jonathan Ferdon for the San Francisco Public Administrator. Paul Malone for Edmund Carney. Jim Ward in Propria Persona. Phillip Harmon appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Mr. Ferdon stated that his position was neutral as to any type of petition to determine heirship. In addition, he stated that he got nominations from a group of Cousins, that the Parties sent affidavits of heirship, that he wanted it to be an even playing field and that he does not represent the Heirs as to there interests. Mr. Malone stated that four Attorney's agreed that there were 12 lines of heirship. The Court informed Mr. Ward that he did not have the authority to speak on behalf of the other interested Parties. Mr. Ward stated that his Father was listed on the petition and requested time to provide death and birth certificates. In addition, he stated that he provided a list of lineal relatives to Mr. Ferdon. The Court suggested Jim Ward Sr. obtain Counsel. The matter was continued to July 8, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. All information is to be provided to all Counsel.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Maria Lertora, CSR#11935. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.
